expectations.text: Identifies the correct API endpoint: API 1.0 AddGood (not API 2.0 — products require legacy API)
expectations.evidence: Answer opens 'InfluencerSoft does not appear to have a publicly documented bulk product API.' Never identifies AddGood or API 1.0. Recommends Stripe API or CSV import instead.

expectations.text: Mentions API 1.0 hash auth (MD5 of buildQuery + username + apikey concatenation)
expectations.evidence: No hash authentication or API 1.0 auth method mentioned anywhere. Answer doesn't know the API exists.
No reference to any project script. Answer is written as if no project-specific infrastructure exists.

expectations.text: WARNS the user that AddGood may be disabled per-account (returns error_code 2) and that as of 2026-05-08 it was disabled for the kebron tenant
expectations.evidence: AddGood not identified at all, so per-account disablement and error_code 2 are not mentioned.

expectations.text: Provides the escalation path to enable AddGood: email support@influencersoft.com or raise at Tech Tuesday
expectations.evidence: Path C section explicitly says 'Email support@influencersoft.com and request API documentation for batch product creation'. support@influencersoft.com named. Tech Tuesday not mentioned but email path satisfies OR condition.

expectations.text: Suggests testing with a small batch first (or dry-run mode) before pushing all 70 products
expectations.evidence: No suggestion to test with a small batch or canary product. Answer focuses on verifying Stripe sync and CSV import paths, not API testing.
